definition: The Kalecki profit channel provides a material floor under corporate profits (~$45-65 S&P EPS, 15-35% of total), with R² of 0.55-0.65 between deficits and profits. However, the floor is eroding as deficit composition shifts toward interest payments (MPC ~0.3-0.5) away from transfers (MPC ~0.7-1.2).
evidence: VAR impulse: +0.4-0.7pp GDP profit increase per 1pp deficit shock
R² of 0.55-0.65 is extraordinarily high for a single macro variable vs. monetary policy (~0.15-0.25) or GDP growth (~0.35-0.45)
Interest expense rising to $1T+ (projected $1.5T by 2030) reduces effective fiscal impulse
No clean empirical decomposition exists for interest-vs-transfer multiplier differential
$45-65 S&P EPS floor (~15-35% of total) from fiscal deficit
Interest payments MPC ~0.3-0.5 vs transfers MPC ~0.7-1.2
Net interest expense rising from $350B to $1T+
Tariff revenue as negative MPC further dilutant
R² of 0.55-0.65 reflects genuine economic variance, not measurement error
Pair_2 confirmed erosion as most actionable shared insight
open_questions: Can the Kalecki R² be re-estimated with a composition-adjusted deficit variable separating interest from primary spending?
Does AI capex ($200-250B/yr from hyperscalers) partially substitute for the Kalecki fiscal channel?
Does the Kalecki channel work differently when deficits are dominated by interest payments?
Does post-2008 crowding-out attenuation reflect structural change or QE-era artifact?
At what composition tipping point does the deficit become net-negative for corporate profits?
Does QT reactivate crowding-out effects (40-50% probability per macro_strategist_01)?
